sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The cross markers are 3x3 crosses:
Top-right (rows 9-11, cols 51-53):
```
BBrBB
BrBrB
BBrBB
```
Bottom-left (rows 51-53, cols 9-11):
```
BBrBB
BrBrB
BBrBB
```
So they're 3x3 crosses with a center pixel at (10, 52) for top-right and (52, 10) for bottom-left.
I clicked at (9, 52) and (10, 52) for the top-right cross, and at (50, 10) for the bottom-left cross. The center pixels are at (10, 52) and (52, 10). I clicked at (10, 52) which is the center of the top-right cross, and it was no-impact. I clicked at (50, 10) which is NOT the center of the bottom-left cross (the center is at 52, 10). Let me try clicking at (52, 10), the center of the bottom-left cross.
But wait, I also need to check if the cross markers are connected objects or separate pixels. Looking at the segmentation, I see multiple r (red) nodes with 1 pixel each. The cross markers might be 5 separate red pixels (center + 4 arms) rather than one connected object.
Actually, looking at the ASCII:
```
BBrBB
BrBrB
BBrBB
```
The center is at (10, 52), and the arms are at (9, 52), (10, 51), (10, 53), (11, 52). These are NOT 4-connected to each other (they're diagonally connected but not orthogonally connected). So the cross marker is 5 separate red pixels, not one connected object.
I clicked at (9, 52) which is the top arm of the top-right cross, and it was no-impact. I clicked at (10, 52) which is the center, and it was no-impact. Let me try clicking at (52, 10), the center of the bottom-left cross.
Actually wait, I already tried (10, 52) and it was no-impact. Let me try (52, 10).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking the center of the bottom-left cross marker
# Center is at (52, 10)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 52, 'col': 10}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
